concepts.name: Linked List Basics
concepts.short_definition: Understanding the fundamental structure and operations of a linked list.

concepts.name: Node Structure
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node points to the next node in the sequence.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the first node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the last node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node to a linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: Removing a node from a linked list.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a linked list sequentially.

concepts.name: Stack Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: The conceptual definition of a stack, emphasizing its LIFO behavior.

concepts.name: LIFO Principle
concepts.short_definition: The core principle of a stack where the most recently added item is the first to be removed.

concepts.name: Stack Operations
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental operations performed on a stack: push, pop, peek, isEmpty.

concepts.name: Push Operation
concepts.short_definition: Adding an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop Operation
concepts.short_definition: Removing and returning the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek Operation
concepts.short_definition: Returning the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: isEmpty Operation
concepts.short_definition: Checking if the stack contains any elements.

concepts.name: Stack Implementation
concepts.short_definition: How to build a stack data structure using underlying components.

concepts.name: Stack using Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a stack data structure using a linked list as the underlying storage.

concepts.name: Top Pointer for Stack
concepts.short_definition: A pointer in the linked list implementation that always points to the top element of the stack.

concepts.name: Push with Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the push operation by adding a new node at the beginning of the linked list.

concepts.name: Pop with Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the pop operation by removing the node at the beginning of the linked list.

concepts.name: Peek with Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the peek operation by returning the data from the node pointed to by the top pointer.

concepts.name: isEmpty with Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Implementing isEmpty by checking if the top pointer is null.

concepts.name: Time Complexity of LL Stack
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the efficiency of stack operations when implemented with a linked list.

concepts.name: Space Complexity of LL Stack
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the memory usage of a stack implemented with a linked list.

concepts.name: Advantages of LL Stack
concepts.short_definition: The benefits of using a linked list to implement a stack, such as dynamic sizing.

concepts.name: Disadvantages of LL Stack
concepts.short_definition: The potential drawbacks of using a linked list to implement a stack, such as overhead.

concepts.name: Comparison with Array Stack
concepts.short_definition: Comparing the linked list implementation of a stack with an array-based implementation.

concepts.name: Dynamic Sizing
concepts.short_definition: The ability of the data structure to grow or shrink in size as needed.

concepts.name: Memory Overhead
concepts.short_definition: The extra memory used by pointers in a linked list implementation.

concepts.name: Edge Cases
concepts.short_definition: Scenarios like an empty stack or a stack with a single element.

concepts.name: Null Pointer Handling
concepts.short_definition: Safely managing operations when pointers might be null.

concepts.name: Garbage Collection
concepts.short_definition: How memory is managed and reclaimed in a linked list implementation.

concepts.name: Recursive Stack Operations
concepts.short_definition: Implementing stack operations using recursion (though less common for basic stack ops).

concepts.name: Applications of Stack
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios where stacks are utilized.

concepts.name: Function Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: The internal stack used by programming languages to manage function calls.

concepts.name: Expression Evaluation
concepts.short_definition: Using stacks to convert and evaluate mathematical expressions.

concepts.name: Backtracking Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Employing stacks in algorithms that explore multiple possibilities.

concepts.name: Undo/Redo Functionality
concepts.short_definition: Implementing undo and redo features using stacks.

concepts.name: Syntax Parsing
concepts.short_definition: Using stacks in compilers to analyze the structure of code.
